verbatim_quote: The gene encoding the secreted 53-kDa metalloprotease (protease B) and the 5' end of the gene encoding the secreted 55-kDa metalloprotease (protease C) of the Gram-negative bacterium Erwinia chrysanthemi have been sequenced. The predicted sequences of the two proteases do not have typical signal sequences at their NH2 termini. Both proteases are synthesized as inactive higher molecular weight precursors (zymogens proB and proC) which are secreted into the external medium where divalent cation-mediated activation occurs.
notes: Delepelaire & Wandersman 1989 JBC: primary characterization of E. chrysanthemi protease B secreted signal-peptide-independently (T1SS/ABC route); current lipase DOI did not name prtB.

verbatim_quote: One of the secreted proteins is PrtA, which is shown here to be a repeats-in-toxin (RTX) alkaline zinc metalloprotease... The prtA gene resides in a locus encoding both the protease ABC transporter (prtBCD) and an intervening ORF encoding a protease inhibitor (inh).
notes: Bowen et al. 2003: Photorhabdus PrtA secreted via its ABC transporter (prtBCD = T1SS). Row gene 'prtA1' vs paper 'prtA' (same protease); current lipase DOI did not cover Photorhabdus.

verbatim_quote: The extracellular alkaline protease produced by Pseudomonas aeruginosa is secreted by a specific pathway, independent of the pathway used by most of the other extracellular proteins of this organism. Secretion of this protease is dependent on the presence of several genes located adjacent to the apr gene... The secretion functions involved in alpha-haemolysin secretion in E. coli (hlyB, hlyD, tolC) can also be used to complement alkaline protease secretion by E. coli.
notes: Guzzo et al. 1991: P. aeruginosa alkaline protease (aprA) secreted by HlyB-family ABC/T1SS apparatus; current lipase DOI did not name aprA.

P. entomophila AprA is experimentally shown secreted (Liehl 2006, doi 10.1371/journal.ppat.0020056) but no primary paper experimentally demonstrates T1SS/ABC export of the P. entomophila ortholog specifically; T1SS assignment is by homology to P. aeruginosa AprA.

verbatim_quote: As for E. coli alpha-haemolysin, secretion of B.pertussis adenylate cyclase and haemolysin requires the expression of additional genes... requiring, in addition to the cyaB and cyaD gene products, the presence of a third gene product specified by the cyaE gene.
notes: Glaser et al. 1988 EMBO J: CyaA secreted by cyaBDE T1SS. Primary experimental work is in B. pertussis; this row is the B. bronchiseptica ortholog (cyaABDE operon conserved). Current E-cadherin DOI was wrong-topic.

verbatim_quote: As for E. coli alpha-haemolysin, secretion of B.pertussis adenylate cyclase and haemolysin requires the expression of additional genes... requiring, in addition to the cyaB and cyaD gene products, the presence of a third gene product specified by the cyaE gene.
notes: Glaser et al. 1988 EMBO J: B. pertussis CyaA (cyclolysin) secreted by cyaBDE T1SS (HlyB/HlyD homologues). Exact organism match. Current E-cadherin DOI was wrong-topic.
